channel.title: Sovereign on Adler Medrado's corner of the web
channel.description: Recent content in Sovereign on Adler Medrado's corner of the web

item.title: X reactivated my account
item.description: After being suspended without warning, my account on X was restored. Lesson stays the same: if you rely only on centralized platforms, you are always one click away from being erased.

item.title: They suspended my X account
item.description: Account from 2006. Almost 1200 followers. GONE. No clear reason. just “violated authenticity rules.” Didn’t insult anyone. Didn’t spread fake news. Just posted. They threatened to block any new account I create. See how dumb it is to rely on centralized platforms?

item.title: Building My Own Sovereign RAG for Secure Code Analysis
item.description: Building My Own Sovereign RAG for Secure Code Analysis Lately, I’ve been taking a closer look at some code analysis tools that claim to detect security vulnerabilities in software projects. The idea itself is solid. I got one of these tools recommended to me and decided to dig deeper to see what’s really behind these solutions. Pretty quickly I noticed a pattern: these platforms are far from cheap. Some offer limited free plans, but we all know how this game works.
